metadata.summary: Privacy-first, cookie-free web analytics for Gatsby, React, and static sites without GDPR consent overhead. Deploy the Nginx proxy before launch to ensure 20-40% of adblocker users remain visible.

content.title: Plausible Analytics
content.summary: Privacy-first, cookie-free web analytics for Gatsby, React, and static sites. Covers script installation, Nginx proxy setup for adblocker bypass, custom event tracking, and the Stats API for programmatic data access. The core rule: deploy the Nginx proxy before launch — without it, adblocker users (20-40% of technical audiences) are invisible.

why.p: Google Analytics requires GDPR cookie consent, adds ~45KB to page weight, and sends user data to Google. Plausible is GDPR-compliant by design (no cookies, no personal data), lightweight (less than 1KB script), and exposes a REST Stats API that agents can query programmatically for reports, dashboards, and automated alerts.

list.item: Setting up privacy-first analytics on a Gatsby, React, or static site without consent banners
Replacing Google Analytics where cookie consent overhead is undesirable
Querying site metrics programmatically to feed reports or agent decisions
Tracking custom conversion events (signups, purchases, CTA clicks) via Stats API
Self-hosting analytics for full data ownership
You need user-level session replay or heatmaps — Plausible is aggregate-only; use PostHog
You need funnel analysis across authenticated user sessions — use Mixpanel or PostHog
You require advanced cohort analysis or A/B test results — Plausible lacks these
You need advertising attribution with click IDs (gclid, fbclid) mapped to conversions

section.name: script-variants
section.p: Plausible's script is less than 1KB in its base form. The all-extensions variant adds file-downloads, outbound-links, and tagged-events tracking, increasing size and complexity. Choose the script variant based on the features required and do not use the all-extensions variant by default — include only what is needed to keep the script minimal.

list.item: Basic: script.js — pageview tracking only
With custom events: script.tagged-events.js
With file downloads: script.file-downloads.js
All extensions: script.tagged-events.file-downloads.outbound-links.js

example.title: Basic pageview tracking
example.code:
<!-- Basic pageview tracking --> <script defer data-domain="yourdomain.com" src="https://plausible.io/js/script.js"></script>
<!-- With custom events (use this for conversion tracking) --> <script defer data-domain="yourdomain.com" src="https://plausible.io/js/script.tagged-events.js"></script>

section.name: nginx-proxy-adblocker-bypass
section.p: Plausible's default script URL (plausible.io) is blocked by most adblockers. A proxied script via the site's own domain bypasses these blocks, restoring data accuracy for privacy-conscious technical users. Deploy the Nginx proxy before launch on any site targeting a technical audience. Without it, 20-40% of users running adblockers are invisible.

rule.statement: Deploy the Nginx proxy before launch on any site targeting a technical audience. Without it, 20-40% of users running adblockers are invisible.
rule.rationale: Plausible's default script URL (plausible.io) is blocked by most adblockers. A proxied script via the site's own domain bypasses these blocks, restoring data accuracy for privacy-conscious technical users.

example.title: Using proxied script after Nginx proxy setup
example.code: <!-- Use proxied script after Nginx proxy setup --> <script defer data-domain="yourdomain.com" data-api="/api/event" src="/js/script.js"></script>

template.purpose: Nginx proxy configuration for adblocker bypass

section.name: framework-integrations
section.p: In Next.js and Gatsby, guard plausible() calls with typeof window !== 'undefined' to prevent SSR errors. Plausible's JavaScript function runs in the browser only. SSR environments do not have a window object. Calling plausible() during server-side rendering throws a ReferenceError.

rule.statement: In Next.js and Gatsby, guard plausible() calls with typeof window !== 'undefined' to prevent SSR errors.
rule.rationale: Plausible's JavaScript function runs in the browser only. SSR environments do not have a window object. Calling plausible() during server-side rendering throws a ReferenceError.

section.name: development-exclusion
section.p: Development visits that appear in analytics data distort traffic numbers. The localStorage flag is persistent across sessions and survives page reloads.

rule.statement: Exclude own visits during development by setting localStorage.setItem('plausible_ignore', 'true') in the browser console.
rule.rationale: Development visits that appear in analytics data distort traffic numbers. The localStorage flag is persistent across sessions and survives page reloads.

section.name: custom-events-and-goal-tracking
section.p: Plausible requires goals to be pre-registered in the dashboard settings. Firing events without corresponding goal definitions produces no data in the dashboard.

rule.statement: Define goals in the Plausible dashboard before firing events from code — events that do not match a defined goal are silently ignored.
rule.rationale: Plausible requires goals to be pre-registered in the dashboard settings. Firing events without corresponding goal definitions produces no data in the dashboard.

example.title: Basic conversion event with custom properties

section.name: css-class-tagged-events-no-code-tracking
section.p: Tagged events reduce deployment friction: a designer can add a tracking class in HTML without touching JavaScript. This is also more resilient to JS bundle changes.

rule.statement: Use CSS class tagged events for CTA click tracking when possible — no JavaScript code changes needed when adding new buttons.
rule.rationale: Tagged events reduce deployment friction: a designer can add a tracking class in HTML without touching JavaScript. This is also more resilient to JS bundle changes.

example.title: CSS class tagged events for CTA tracking
example.code: View Pricing

section.name: revenue-tracking-note
section.p: Revenue tracking silently fails on plans that do not support it. Verifying the plan before implementation avoids lost revenue data without error messages.

rule.statement: Verify the Plausible plan supports revenue tracking before implementing — this feature is only available in the hosted Business plan, not in Community Edition (CE).
rule.rationale: Revenue tracking silently fails on plans that do not support it. Verifying the plan before implementation avoids lost revenue data without error messages.

section.name: api-endpoints-and-stats-api
section.p: API endpoints provide programmatic access to analytics data. Agents can extract metrics, build reports, and trigger automated actions based on analytics trends.

list.item: Aggregate: /api/v1/stats/aggregate — overall metrics for a period
Timeseries: /api/v1/stats/timeseries — daily breakdown for a period
Breakdown: /api/v1/stats/breakdown — grouped by property (page, source, device)
Realtime: /api/v1/stats/realtime/visitors — current visitors (last 5 min)

example.title: Aggregate stats API query
example.code:
# Aggregate stats — last 30 days curl "https://plausible.io/api/v1/stats/aggregate?site_id=yourdomain.com&period=30d&metrics=visitors,pageviews,bounce_rate" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $PLAUSIBLE_API_KEY"
# Top pages breakdown curl "https://plausible.io/api/v1/stats/breakdown?site_id=yourdomain.com&period=30d&property=event:page&metrics=visitors,pageviews" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $PLAUSIBLE_API_KEY"

rule.statement: Use exact period parameter values: day, 7d, 30d, month, 6mo, 12mo, or YYYY-MM-DD,YYYY-MM-DD. Non-standard formats return errors.
rule.rationale: Agents frequently use incorrect period formats (e.g., "last_30_days", "30_days", "1m") that are not valid for the Plausible API and produce 400 errors.

rule.statement: Use exact property values in breakdown queries. Valid examples: event:page, visit:source, visit:device, visit:country.
rule.rationale: Agents hallucinate property names based on what "seems right" (e.g., "page:url", "traffic:source"). Only properties in the official API documentation work.

section.name: python-client
section.p: The Stats API is fully headless and agent-compatible — no browser interaction required. Use it from server-side scripts and agent pipelines directly. Unlike most analytics tools, Plausible's Stats API does not require OAuth or browser sessions. A simple Bearer token is sufficient for all read operations.

rule.statement: The Stats API is fully headless and agent-compatible — no browser interaction required. Use it from server-side scripts and agent pipelines directly.
rule.rationale: Unlike most analytics tools, Plausible's Stats API does not require OAuth or browser sessions. A simple Bearer token is sufficient for all read operations.

template.purpose: Python client with aggregate(), timeseries(), breakdown(), realtime_visitors() methods

list.item: No user-level or session-level queries — aggregate only by design
Custom properties limited on free/self-hosted plan
Revenue tracking requires hosted Business plan
Raw event export (Data Export API) requires Business/Enterprise plan
Rate limits apply — paginate for large breakdowns

template.purpose: LLM prompt to generate a weekly stats summary from API data
LLM prompt to generate custom event tracking code for a site
